title: FMCSA Violations After a Truck Crash in Fresno, California
twitter: 🚛 Hit by a truck in Fresno? FMCSA violations—hours-of-service, bad brakes, unqualified drivers—could be the key to your case. California's 2-year clock is ticking. Call 855-WRECKMATCH (855-897-3256) now. https://www.wreckmatch.com/blog/fmcsa-violations-after-a-truck-crash-in-fresno-california
linkedin:
If you or someone you represent was involved in a commercial truck crash in Fresno, California, federal FMCSA regulations may be central to determining liability—and most victims don't realize it.

FMCSA violations fall into three major categories that attorneys routinely investigate after a crash: hours-of-service violations (was the driver fatigued beyond legal limits?), vehicle maintenance failures (were brakes, tires, or systems neglected?), and driver qualification gaps (did the carrier hire someone unfit to operate a commercial vehicle?).

In California, the statute of limitations for personal injury claims is generally two years from the date of the accident. That window sounds generous—but trucking companies begin preserving (or quietly losing) records immediately after a crash. Early legal action can mean the difference between a complete evidentiary record and a case built on gaps.

WreckMatch connects injured parties and their families with our network of 800+ vetted personal injury law firms nationwide, including attorneys who specialize in commercial truck litigation in the Central Valley and across California.

If FMCSA violations played a role in your Fresno truck accident, don't wait. Connect with a qualified attorney today: https://www.wreckmatch.com/#form

If a commercial truck hit you in Fresno, the legal side of things can feel overwhelming—especially when you're still recovering. But here's something important most people don't know: federal FMCSA rules govern every commercial truck on the road, and when carriers or drivers break those rules, it can be powerful evidence in your case.

Violations like pushing drivers past legal hours-of-service limits, skipping required vehicle inspections, or hiring unqualified drivers are unfortunately more common than they should be. And in California, you generally have two years from the date of the accident to file a claim—but trucking companies start building their defense immediately.

reddit_title: Truck accident in Fresno, CA — how do FMCSA violations affect my injury claim?
reddit_body:
If you were hit by a commercial truck in Fresno or anywhere in California's Central Valley, it's worth understanding that federal FMCSA regulations govern those drivers and carriers—and violations of those rules can significantly strengthen an injury claim. The big three to look into are hours-of-service violations (fatigued driving), maintenance failures (brakes, tires, lights), and driver qualification issues (improper licensing or background checks skipped by the carrier).

California has a 2-year statute of limitations for personal injury claims, but don't treat that as breathing room. Trucking companies and their insurers start documenting and sometimes purging records quickly after a crash. An attorney can send a spoliation letter early on to preserve black box data, driver logs, and maintenance records—evidence that can be critical to your case.

If you're not sure where to find a truck accident attorney who actually knows FMCSA law, wreckmatch.com connects accident victims with vetted lawyers across California and nationwide. It's a free way to get matched without having to cold-call firms hoping someone picks up.
